mechanism_narrative: Positive real activity momentum in retail sales signals improving household cash-flow expectations and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS is trending upward.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS value in snapshot is 447255.0
failure_condition: Reject if higher trailing 3-month RSAFS growth does not predict higher 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, with Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of FEDFUNDS above a low-rate threshold marks the start of tighter discount-rate conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% after being below 0.5% within the prior 6 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value in snapshot is 0.12
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below untriggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll expansion and falling unemployment identify broad labor-market strengthening that raises expected household income and aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:latest PAYEMS value in snapshot is 142654.0
fact:latest UNRATE value in snapshot is 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either single-series signal and has a 20-day forward-return hit rate at or below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and operating leverage, causing downward revisions to earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO value in snapshot is 107.0917
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the downside hit rate is below 55% over the full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weaker interest-sensitive demand and reduced future construction activity, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST value in snapshot is 1206.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST weakness events have higher mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-events or if the estimated return spread is not negative in at least 70% of rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while policy rates remain near zero increases inflation uncertainty and future tightening risk, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates by more than 0.5pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest CPIAUCSL value in snapshot is 237.566
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value in snapshot is 0.12
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and low FEDFUNDS interaction has no negative incremental effect relative to CPIAUCSL alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP momentum captures broad aggregate demand growth that supports corporate revenues and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when GDPC1 growth is rising.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:latest GDPC1 value in snapshot is 16394.2
failure_condition: Reject if higher trailing quarterly GDPC1 growth is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 forward returns after real-time data lags, with Rank IC below 0.02 across two consecutive expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and investor risk appetite, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE value in snapshot is 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE jump events are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below the unconditional 5-day mean.
